Issuing Office: UIDAI (via Aadhaar Seva Kendra/enrolment centre)
Fee: Free for enrolment/update of demographic details; Rs 50 for biometric update, Rs 50 for PVC card
Processing Time: 90 days for first enrolment (typical); updates 1-90 days
Online Process: Book a slot at an Aadhaar Seva Kendra via appointments.uidai.gov.in (new enrolment/update appointment; biometric enrolment cannot be fully online). Address-only updates (no biometric change) can be done fully online via myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in using OTP + existing Aadhaar.
Offline Process: Visit nearest Aadhaar Seva Kendra / bank-run Aadhaar centre / post office Aadhaar counter / Karnataka One / Bangalore One centre. Fill enrolment/update form, biometric capture (photo, iris, 10 fingerprints) for new enrolment, submit ID+address proof, collect acknowledgement slip with URN.
Required Documents: New enrolment: any 1 valid PoI (Passport/PAN/Voter ID/DL) + 1 valid PoA (utility bill/bank statement/ration card) OR birth certificate for minors OR proof from head of family. Update: existing Aadhaar + supporting proof for the field being changed.

Issuing Office: Income Tax Dept (via NSDL/Protean or UTIITSL)
Fee: Rs 107 (physical dispatch in India) / Rs 72 (e-PAN only) via Protean or UTIITSL
Processing Time: 15-20 days for physical card; e-PAN within a few days
Online Process: Apply via Protean (formerly NSDL) e-Gov portal or UTIITSL portal; fill Form 49A (resident)/49AA (foreign), upload photo/signature/documents, e-sign or send physical acknowledgement, pay fee online.
Offline Process: Download Form 49A, fill manually, submit with documents and DD/fee at nearest Protean TIN-Facilitation Centre or UTIITSL centre (several in Bengaluru and other Karnataka cities).
Required Documents: Proof of identity, proof of address, proof of date of birth (Aadhaar commonly used for all three), passport-size photo (for physical form).

Issuing Office: Electoral Registration Officer (ERO) of the Assembly Constituency, via Karnataka Chief Electoral Officer
Department: Election Commission of India (via Karnataka CEO)
Fee: Free
Processing Time: Typically 30 days (Form 6 new enrolment)
Online Process: Apply via the Voter Helpline app or voters.eci.gov.in - Form 6 for new registration/shifting from another constituency, Form 8 for correction, upload photo and supporting documents; application routed to the local ERO/BLO for field verification.
Offline Process: Submit filled Form 6/8 with documents to the local Booth Level Officer (BLO) or the ERO's office (Taluk/constituency office) in Karnataka; a physical verification visit by the BLO is standard before approval.
Required Documents: Proof of age (birth certificate/10th marksheet), proof of ordinary residence (Aadhaar/utility bill/rent agreement), passport-size photo.

Issuing Office: Regional Passport Office (RPO) Bengaluru, via Passport Seva Kendra (PSK)/Post Office PSK network in Karnataka
Fee: Rs 1,500 (normal, 36-page, adult) / Rs 3,500 (Tatkal)
Processing Time: Normal: ~30-45 days; Tatkal: 1-3 days after police verification/appointment
Online Process: Register on passportindia.gov.in, fill the online application form, pay fee online, book an appointment at the nearest Passport Seva Kendra (PSK) or Post Office PSK (POPSK) under RPO Bengaluru's jurisdiction.
Offline Process: Visit the booked PSK/POPSK in person with originals + self-attested photocopies for document verification, photo and biometric capture; police verification (pre- or post-issuance) conducted by local Karnataka Police.
Required Documents: Proof of Date of Birth, proof of address (Aadhaar/utility bill), Aadhaar (for address auto-fetch/Tatkal), Annexure forms as applicable (e.g. for minors, name change).

Issuing Office: Regional Transport Office (RTO)/Assistant RTO
Fee: Learner's Licence: ~Rs 200-250; Permanent DL: ~Rs 200-700 depending on vehicle class (fees revised periodically by MoRTH/state)
Processing Time: Learner's Licence: same day after test; Permanent DL: issued after road test, typically within a few days of the test slot
Online Process: Apply via sarathi.parivahan.gov.in - Learner's Licence application with online test slot booking (LL test itself is computer-based at the RTO); after 30 days, apply for Permanent DL and book a road-test slot at the jurisdictional Karnataka RTO.
Offline Process: Visit the jurisdictional RTO/ARTO in person for the LL computer test and later the Permanent DL road test; document verification and biometrics/photo capture are done in person regardless of online booking.
Required Documents: Proof of age (birth certificate/10th marksheet), proof of address (Aadhaar/utility bill), passport-size photo, medical certificate (Form 1A) for transport/hazardous vehicle categories or applicants above 40.

Issuing Office: Regional Transport Office (RTO)/Assistant RTO
Fee: Registration fee + road tax (one-time, lifetime for non-transport vehicles) - amount depends on vehicle category, price and engine capacity
Processing Time: Typically 7-15 days after document/inspection verification
Online Process: Dealer typically initiates registration via Vahan at the time of sale using the invoice, temporary registration and insurance details; owner can also track/apply for renewal or transfer online via vahan.parivahan.gov.in.
Offline Process: Physical inspection of the vehicle is mandatory at the jurisdictional RTO for new registration; document submission and RC collection can also be done in person at the RTO counter.
Required Documents: Vehicle purchase invoice, PAN Card (or Form 60), address proof, vehicle insurance, Form 20/21/22 as applicable, road-worthiness/emission certificate.

Issuing Office: Tahsildar / AC (Assistant Commissioner) / Food & Civil Supplies Inspector, via Nadakacheri/Ahara
Department: Karnataka Food, Civil Supplies & Consumer Affairs Department
Fee: Nominal (Rs 10-50 range via Nadakacheri/CSC)
Processing Time: 30-45 days (as per Karnataka Sakala/RTS Act norms)
Online Process: Apply via ahara.kar.nic.in or the Nadakacheri/Seva Sindhu portal - fill household details, upload Aadhaar of all members and income/residence proof, submit for verification by the local Food Inspector.
Offline Process: Submit application with documents at the local Taluk Food & Civil Supplies office, Nadakacheri Kendra, or Karnataka One/Bangalore One centre.
Required Documents: Aadhaar of all family members, proof of residence, family photograph, income proof (for BPL/Priority category), old ration card (if shifting/updating).

Issuing Office: Tahsildar / Food & Civil Supplies Dept (merged into the Ration Card categorisation itself in Karnataka)
Department: Karnataka Food, Civil Supplies & Consumer Affairs Department
Fee: Nominal (bundled with ration card fee)
Processing Time: Bundled with ration card processing (30-45 days)
Online Process: Not a separate online application - BPL/Priority Household status is determined during Ration Card application/survey on ahara.kar.nic.in based on income and NFSA eligibility criteria.
Offline Process: Same as Ration Card: Aadhaar of all members, income proof, proof of residence.
Required Documents: Same as Ration Card: Aadhaar of all members, income proof, proof of residence.

Issuing Office: Tahsildar (via Nadakacheri/Atalji Janasnehi Kendra)
Fee: Nominal (Rs 15-25 via Nadakacheri/AJSK)
Processing Time: Typically 7-21 working days
Online Process: Apply via nadakacheri.karnataka.gov.in (AJSK portal) - select the caste certificate service, fill applicant/family details, upload supporting documents, pay fee online; routed to the Village Accountant/Revenue Inspector then Tahsildar for verification and digital signature.
Offline Process: Submit application with documents at the nearest Nadakacheri Kendra / Atalji Janasnehi Kendra / Karnataka One centre in the applicant's taluk.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, parent's/relative's existing caste certificate (if available) or School Leaving Certificate showing caste, residence proof, affidavit for cases lacking documentary proof.

Issuing Office: Tahsildar (via Nadakacheri)
Fee: Nominal (Rs 15-25 via Nadakacheri)
Processing Time: 7-21 working days
Online Process: Apply via nadakacheri.karnataka.gov.in, select Domicile/Residence certificate, upload residence proof spanning the required years, submit for Tahsildar verification and digital signature.
Offline Process: Submit application with documents at the local Nadakacheri Kendra / Taluk office.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, proof of continuous residence in Karnataka (ration card, voter ID, property documents, or school records for the required duration), affidavit if documentary proof is incomplete.

Issuing Office: Tahsildar (via Nadakacheri)
Fee: Nominal (Rs 15-25 via Nadakacheri)
Processing Time: 7-21 working days (Karnataka RTS Act norms)
Online Process: Apply via nadakacheri.karnataka.gov.in, select Income Certificate, enter income/family details, upload supporting documents, submit for Village Accountant/Revenue Inspector verification and Tahsildar's digital signature.
Offline Process: Submit application with documents at the local Nadakacheri Kendra / Taluk office.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, salary slips/Form 16 (salaried) or self-declaration/affidavit (self-employed/agricultural income), proof of residence.

Issuing Office: Tahsildar (via Nadakacheri)
Fee: Nominal (Rs 15-25 via Nadakacheri)
Processing Time: 7-21 working days
Online Process: Apply via nadakacheri.karnataka.gov.in, select EWS Certificate, enter family income and asset details as per the central EWS criteria, upload proof, submit for Tahsildar verification.
Offline Process: Submit application with documents at the local Nadakacheri Kendra / Taluk office.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, income certificate/proof, land/property holding declaration, caste certificate (to confirm applicant is not already covered under SC/ST/OBC reservation).

Issuing Office: District Medical Board (assessment, at District Hospital) + DEPwD (central UDID portal)
Department: Karnataka Health & Family Welfare Dept (Medical Board) + DEPwD (Central)
Fee: Free (UDID registration and certificate issuance)
Processing Time: Assessment appointment + certificate issuance can take several weeks depending on the District Medical Board's camp schedule
Online Process: Register on swavlambancard.gov.in, fill personal and disability details, upload photo and existing medical records; system schedules/refers the applicant to the nearest Karnataka District Medical Board for physical assessment.
Offline Process: Attend the District Medical Board assessment in person at the district hospital (or a disability assessment camp); certificate and UDID card issued after the board's evaluation and portal upload.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, existing medical records/reports related to the disability, passport-size photo, proof of residence.

Issuing Office: Tahsildar (via Nadakacheri) / Dept for Empowerment of Differently Abled and Senior Citizens
Department: Karnataka Revenue Dept / Dept for Empowerment of Differently Abled and Senior Citizens
Fee: Nominal (Rs 15-25 via Nadakacheri)
Processing Time: 7-21 working days
Online Process: Apply via nadakacheri.karnataka.gov.in or sevasindhu.karnataka.gov.in, select Senior Citizen Certificate/Card, enter age proof details, upload documents, submit for Tahsildar verification and digital signature.
Offline Process: Submit application with documents at the local Nadakacheri Kendra / Taluk office / Karnataka One centre.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card (age proof), proof of residence in Karnataka, passport-size photo.

Issuing Office: Sub-Registrar
Department: Karnataka Stamps & Registration Department (via Kaveri Online Services)
Fee: Stamp duty: slab-based, roughly 2-5% of property value plus surcharge/cess in urban areas; Registration fee: 2% of property value (revised effective around August 2025)
Processing Time: Same-day registration once SRO appointment slot is completed
Online Process: Register on kaverionline.karnataka.gov.in, enter property and party details, use the built-in stamp duty & registration fee calculator, pay via the K2 payment system, book a Sub-Registrar Office (SRO) appointment slot.
Offline Process: Visit the booked SRO in person on the appointment date with both parties and witnesses for biometric verification, document execution and signatures; physical presence at the SRO remains mandatory regardless of online booking.
Required Documents: Sale agreement, identity/address proof of buyer and seller, PAN of both parties, property tax receipts/khata extract, previous title deed, encumbrance certificate.

Issuing Office: Sub-Registrar (via Kaveri Online e-EC search)
Fee: Nominal search fee (per-year search, typically Rs 30-50 per year searched) plus a small certificate issuance fee
Processing Time: Same-day for e-EC generated online; a few days if manual verification is needed for older records
Online Process: Apply via kaverionline.karnataka.gov.in's EC/e-EC search service, enter property survey/site details and the years to be searched, pay fee online; e-EC downloadable immediately if records are digitised.
Offline Process: Visit the jurisdictional Sub-Registrar Office (SRO) in person to request manual EC search for years/records not yet digitised on Kaveri.
Required Documents: Property details (survey number/site number, village/taluk), years for which EC is required, applicant ID proof.

Issuing Office: Village Accountant / Tahsildar (via Bhoomi)
Fee: Free to view online; digitally signed copy ~Rs 10-15 via Bhoomi Kiosk/Nada Kacheri
Processing Time: Instant view/download online; digitally signed copy typically same-day at a Bhoomi Kiosk
Online Process: View and download the RTC/Pahani for free via landrecords.karnataka.gov.in (Bhoomi Online) by entering district, taluk, hobli, village and survey number; a digitally signed copy can be requested online for a nominal fee.
Offline Process: Visit the local Bhoomi Kiosk (usually at the Taluk office) with survey number details to obtain a printed, signed RTC/Pahani copy.
Required Documents: Survey number/site details, applicant ID proof (for signed copy requests), Aadhaar (for some Bhoomi services requiring authentication).

Issuing Office: Village Accountant / Tahsildar (via Bhoomi Mojini)
Fee: Nominal Bhoomi Kiosk fee (similar to RTC, Rs 10-30 range)
Processing Time: Statutory processing window per Karnataka Land Revenue Act rules
Online Process: Mutation is auto-initiated in the Bhoomi system when a registered Sale Deed is captured via the Kaveri-Bhoomi integration; status can be tracked online via landrecords.karnataka.gov.in.
Offline Process: For inheritance, partition, gift, or non-integrated transactions, apply for mutation (Mojini) in person at the Bhoomi Kiosk/Tahsildar's office of the taluk where the land is located, with supporting title/transfer documents.
Required Documents: Registered Sale Deed / succession certificate / partition deed / gift deed, RTC extract, Aadhaar, applicant's ID proof.

Issuing Office: Greater Bengaluru Authority (GBA, successor to BBMP) for Bengaluru; respective City/Town Municipal Corporation or Council elsewhere in Karnataka
Department: Urban Local Body (GBA in Bengaluru / respective ULB elsewhere)
Fee: Tax amount varies by property zone, built-up area and usage (self-assessment scheme - SAS); a rebate (commonly 5%) applies for full early payment
Processing Time: Payment processed instantly online; physical receipt available immediately on payment
Online Process: Pay via bbmptax.karnataka.gov.in (Bengaluru) - enter PID/application number or old khata details, self-assessment form auto-fills where data exists, pay online via card/net-banking/UPI, download receipt instantly.
Offline Process: Pay at designated bank branches, BBMP/GBA Assistant Revenue Officer (ARO) ward offices, or Bangalore One centres with the property's PID/khata details.
Required Documents: Property PID/khata number, previous tax receipt, sale deed/khata certificate (for new properties), Aadhaar/owner ID.

Issuing Office: Sub-Registrar (for registered agreements) / self-executed on e-Stamp paper for short-tenure agreements
Fee: Stamp duty on rent agreements in Karnataka is a small percentage of average annual rent + deposit
Processing Time: Same-day for e-Stamp paper purchase and notarisation; registration (if opted) same-day at SRO
Online Process: Purchase e-Stamp paper online via the Karnataka e-Stamping system/authorised collection centres, draft the agreement, get it notarised, or use the Kaveri Online Services portal to register if the tenure requires it.
Offline Process: Purchase physical stamp paper from a licensed stamp vendor, execute the agreement, get it notarised at a local notary's office; for registrable tenancies, visit the jurisdictional Sub-Registrar Office in person.
Required Documents: ID/address proof of landlord and tenant, property ownership proof (khata/EC), passport-size photos of both parties, 2 witnesses (for registration).

Issuing Office: Karnataka Real Estate Regulatory Authority (K-RERA)
Department: Karnataka Housing Dept (K-RERA - autonomous statutory authority)
Fee: Registration fee based on project type and area (residential: per sq.m. slab; commercial: higher per sq.m. slab, subject to periodic revision)
Processing Time: Statutory timeline of 30 days for the authority to approve/reject a complete application under RERA rules
Online Process: Apply via rera.karnataka.gov.in, create a promoter/agent login, fill project/agent details, upload project documents (title, sanctioned plans, encumbrance certificate, architect/engineer/CA certificates), pay fee online.
Offline Process: Physical submission of hard-copy documents at the K-RERA office in Bengaluru is generally required alongside/after the online application for verification.
Required Documents: Title deed, sanctioned building plan, encumbrance certificate, project layout and specifications, promoter's PAN/company registration, CA/architect/engineer certificates (Form 1/2/3).

Issuing Office: Cooperative Housing Society / Apartment Owners' Association / RWA
Fee: Society-determined (often free for members in good standing; some societies charge an administrative fee)
Processing Time: Society-determined - can range from same-day to several weeks
Online Process: No standard online process; some societies use their own management software/WhatsApp groups for NOC requests.
Offline Process: Submit a written request to the Society Secretary/Management Committee along with relevant supporting documents (e.g. sale agreement, loan sanction letter) as per the society's internal bylaws.
Required Documents: Proof of ownership/tenancy in the society, no-dues certificate from society accounts, purpose-specific documents (e.g. bank loan sanction letter, sale agreement).

Issuing Office: Jurisdictional GST Officer (State Commercial Taxes Dept or Central CBIC, system-allocated)
Department: GST Dept (dual control - Central + Karnataka Commercial Taxes Dept)
Fee: Free (no government fee for registration)
Processing Time: 3-7 working days (up to 30 days if physical verification needed)
Online Process: Apply via gst.gov.in - Part A (PAN, mobile, email, state) and Part B (business details, promoters, place of business, bank account, authorised signatory) with document upload; Aadhaar authentication and/or biometric verification at a GST Suvidha Kendra may be required.
Offline Process: No separate offline channel; GST Seva Kendras/Facilitation Centres (including in Bengaluru) assist with filling the same online application on the applicant's behalf.
Required Documents: PAN, Aadhaar, proof of business constitution, proof of principal place of business (electricity bill/rent agreement/property tax receipt), bank account proof, photograph, authorisation letter/board resolution.

Issuing Office: Labour Department (Karnataka Shops & Commercial Establishments Act, 1961), via e-Karmika portal
Fee: Fee slab based on number of employees
Processing Time: 3-7 working days typically (department's committed SLA is up to 30 days)
Online Process: Register on ekarmika.karnataka.gov.in, fill Form C with establishment and employee details, upload identity/address/business-constitution documents, pay the employee-slab-based fee online; certificate downloadable on approval.
Offline Process: Submit Form C with documents and fee at the nearest KarnatakaOne Centre if online filing assistance is preferred; the B1 acknowledgement receipt should be retained as proof of submission.
Required Documents: Business Constitution Document (Partnership Deed/Incorporation Certificate/MoA-AoA), identity proof of proprietor/partner/director (Aadhaar/Voter ID/Passport), address proof of establishment (rent agreement/property tax receipt/electricity bill), Kannada name-board photograph (mandatory display requirement).

Issuing Office: Municipal Corporation / City Municipal Council / Town Panchayat (Ward/Licensing Office) - GBA for Bengaluru
Fee: Varies by ULB, trade category and premises area
Processing Time: Varies by ULB - typically 15-30 days where a formal SLA exists
Portal: No single statewide portal; varies by ULB
Online Process: Some Karnataka ULBs have begun listing trade-licence-type services on Seva Sindhu or their own municipal e-governance portals, but this is not uniform.
Offline Process: Submit application with documents (site plan, ownership/rental proof, NOCs as applicable) at the local Ward Office / Municipal Licensing Section / GBA zonal office for Bengaluru.
Required Documents: Proof of premises ownership/tenancy, ID proof of applicant, category-specific NOCs (Fire, Health, Pollution Control Board as applicable), photographs of the premises.

Issuing Office: Self-registration (no approving officer)
Fee: Free (official government portal charges no fee)
Processing Time: Instant (Udyam Registration Number generated immediately on submission)
Online Process: Visit udyamregistration.gov.in, enter Aadhaar of proprietor/partner/director, validate via OTP, enter PAN and GSTIN (auto-fetched from government databases), fill investment/turnover details, submit; Udyam Registration Certificate generated instantly with a dynamic QR code.
Offline Process: No offline application form; Common Service Centres (CSCs) across Karnataka can assist applicants to complete the same online form on their behalf.
Required Documents: Aadhaar of the applicant (proprietor/managing partner/karta/authorised signatory), PAN, GSTIN (if applicable), bank account and business activity details.

Issuing Office: FSSAI (Basic Registration/State Licence) via FoSCoS portal - Karnataka Food Safety Commissionerate for State-tier; Central Licence direct with FSSAI
Department: FSSAI (Central) - Karnataka Food Safety & Standards Dept for Basic/State tier
Fee: Basic Registration: Rs 100/year; State Licence: Rs 2,000-5,000/year (slab-based); Central Licence: Rs 7,500/year
Processing Time: 7-30 days
Online Process: Apply via foscos.fssai.gov.in, select Registration/State Licence/Central Licence based on turnover and business category, upload documents, pay fee online; application routed to the Karnataka Food Safety Dept's Designated/Licensing Officer for the relevant district.
Offline Process: Submit physical Form A/B with documents at the local Karnataka Food Safety office (District Health Office in most districts, BBMP Health Dept for Bengaluru) if online filing assistance is needed.
Required Documents: Photo ID of proprietor/partners, proof of business premises, food safety management plan (for licence tier), water test report (for manufacturing), NOC from municipality (in some categories).

Issuing Office: Profession Tax Officer, Commercial Taxes Department, via e-Prerana portal
Fee: Employer deduction: Rs 200/month per eligible employee (Rs 300 in February), capped at Rs 2,500/year; Self-employed (PTEC): flat Rs 2,500/year
Processing Time: Registration/enrolment approval typically within a few working days of document verification
Online Process: Log in to e-Prerana ('New RC Request' for employers, 'New Enrollment' for self-employed), enter PAN/TAN, validate via OTP, upload documents, submit for PTRC/PTEC. Both certificates are downloadable on the portal once approved.
Offline Process: No physical/manual filing accepted - the department has stated the entire process is online-only via e-Prerana; Karnataka One/CSC centres can assist applicants to complete the same online form.
Required Documents: PAN (and TAN for PTRC), incorporation certificate/business registration proof, address proof of business, employee salary list (for PTRC), GSTIN (if applicable).

Issuing Office: Director General, Karnataka State Fire and Emergency Services (KSFES)
Department: Karnataka State Fire and Emergency Services
Fee: Fee based on building/premises area and occupancy category
Processing Time: Provisional NOC after document/plan submission; final NOC issued after mandatory physical site inspection
Online Process: Register as a public/business user on the KSFES online portal, activate account via OTP, log in and apply for 'No Objection Certificate' - enter applicant, site/plot, project and Professional-on-Record (architect/structural engineer/fire consultant) details, submit for the acknowledgement receipt.
Offline Process: Submit the application with building plans, occupancy certificate and fire-safety system layout in person at the jurisdictional Fire Station/Divisional Fire Officer's office; a physical site inspection by KSFES officials is mandatory before final approval regardless of online filing.
Required Documents: Approved building plan, site/plot ownership documents, fire-safety system layout and equipment list, structural engineer/architect/fire consultant details, in-principle approval from Karnataka Udyog Mitra (for industrial/large commercial projects).

Issuing Office: Deputy Commissioner (Excise) of the district for most retail/hotel/bar licences
Fee: Highly category-dependent
Processing Time: Statutory licence validity is generally 1 year (renewable); Karnataka's 2026 excise policy has introduced longer validity, up to 5 years, for certain categories
Online Process: Some parts of the application (e.g. document submission acknowledgement, e-auction participation) are moving online via the Excise Dept's e-services.
Offline Process: Submit the category-specific application form (e.g. Form CL-9 for bar/restaurant, CL-2 for retail shop) with all supporting documents and fee proof at the office of the Deputy Commissioner (Excise) of the district; physical verification of the premises is mandatory.
Required Documents: Proof of identity/address of applicant, business registration/GST certificate, PAN, floor plan of premises, NOCs from local police/fire department/municipal authority, affidavits/undertakings under Excise rules.

Issuing Office: RBI-regulated Banks / NBFCs (any branch)
Department: Reserve Bank of India (Central, via Central KYC Registry)
Fee: Free
Processing Time: Same-day to 2-3 working days for account activation
Online Process: Most banks now offer video-KYC / Aadhaar-OTP based e-KYC for account opening entirely online through the bank's app or net-banking portal.
Offline Process: Visit any bank branch in Karnataka with original + photocopy of KYC documents; branch officer verifies and uploads to CKYCR.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, photograph, address proof if different from Aadhaar.

Issuing Office: Self-filed via e-filing portal (CPC Bengaluru processes centrally)
Fee: Free (e-filing itself); may involve third-party CA/software fees
Processing Time: Filing is instant; refund processing typically 4-6 weeks after e-verification
Online Process: Log in to incometax.gov.in with PAN, select relevant ITR form, pre-filled data auto-populates from Form 26AS/AIS, fill remaining details, submit and e-verify via Aadhaar OTP/net-banking/EVC.
Offline Process: Utility-based offline filing (JSON upload) is available for select ITR forms, then uploaded via the same portal; there is no manual paper-filing counter for most taxpayers anymore.
Required Documents: PAN, Aadhaar, Form 16/salary slips, bank statements, investment proofs for deductions, Form 26AS/AIS.

Issuing Office: Self-filed via GST portal
Department: GSTN - CBIC (Central) + Karnataka Commercial Taxes Dept
Fee: Free (late fee/interest applies only for delayed filing)
Processing Time: Monthly/quarterly filing per GST calendar; processing is near-instant
Online Process: Log in to gst.gov.in, prepare GSTR-1 (outward supplies) and GSTR-3B (summary return + tax payment) using auto-populated data where available, verify and file using DSC or EVC.
Offline Process: GST Suvidha Providers (GSPs) and GST Facilitation Centres in Karnataka assist businesses to prepare and upload returns via the same portal.
Required Documents: GST Registration Certificate, sales/purchase invoices, e-way bills where applicable, input tax credit reconciliation (GSTR-2B).

Issuing Office: Local Police Station (jurisdiction of the hotel), Karnataka State Police
Fee: Free
Processing Time: Ongoing/continuous compliance requirement; foreigner (C-Form) reporting has a short mandatory reporting window (typically within 24 hours of check-in)
Portal: No single dedicated statewide portal confirmed
Online Process: Where available (mainly larger cities/commissionerates), hotels submit guest ID and stay details via an online police-linked hotel registration system; for foreign nationals, C-Form details are submitted online via the Bureau of Immigration's e-FRRO/C-Form system.
Offline Process: Hotels maintain a physical guest register with ID and address details for police inspection; for foreign nationals, a physical C-Form may be submitted to the local police station/FRRO.
Required Documents: Guest's Aadhaar/Passport/Driving Licence, Hotel's Trade Licence, C-Form details for foreign nationals.

Issuing Office: Greater Bengaluru Authority/Municipal Corporation (Licensing Dept), with Police Department clearance where required
Department: Urban Local Body + Karnataka Police Dept (jurisdiction-dependent)
Fee: Varies by ULB and premises size
Processing Time: Varies by ULB
Online Process: Not confirmed as a uniform online service across Karnataka ULBs this pass.
Offline Process: Submit application with Trade Licence, FSSAI registration, and Fire NOC (where applicable) at the local Municipal Corporation/GBA zonal licensing office; police clearance may be sought separately by the ULB in some jurisdictions.
Required Documents: Trade Licence, FSSAI Registration, Fire NOC, proof of premises ownership/tenancy.

Issuing Office: Employer (registers employee); EPFO issues/maintains UAN
Fee: Free for employees (employer bears statutory contribution matching)
Processing Time: UAN generation is near-instant once employer submits details; KYC seeding can take a few days
Online Process: Employer generates UAN via the Employer/Unified Portal; employee then activates UAN on unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in using UAN + registered mobile OTP, and seeds Aadhaar/PAN/bank KYC online.
Offline Process: For grievances or physical claims, employees can approach the jurisdictional EPFO office in Karnataka (e.g. Bengaluru Regional Office) or use an EPFO facilitation/CSC centre.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, bank account details, employer's PF registration (Establishment Code).

Issuing Office: Employer (registers employee); ESIC issues card
Department: Employees' State Insurance Corporation (Central; dispensary network is Karnataka-specific)
Fee: Free for employee (12/75% - employer/employee statutory contribution)
Processing Time: e-Pehchan card typically issued within a few days of employer registration
Online Process: Employer registers the establishment and employees on esic.gov.in; employee's e-Pehchan card/Temporary Identity Certificate is generated online and can be downloaded/printed immediately.
Offline Process: Employee can visit the nearest ESIC Branch Office in Karnataka to collect a physical Pehchan card or resolve registration issues.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, employer's ESI registration (code number), employee's bank account and family details.

Issuing Office: Registering Officer (Senior/Labour Inspector), Karnataka Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (KBOCWWB), via Seva Sindhu
Department: Karnataka Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
Fee: Nominal registration fee (as prescribed by KBOCWWB)
Processing Time: Verification and approval by Senior/Labour Inspector followed by Labour Officer review
Online Process: Apply via sevasindhu.karnataka.gov.in - search for 'Application for Registration ... Karnataka Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board', fill applicant and employment details, upload Employment Certificate (Form V(A)/V(B)/V(C)/V(D)), Aadhaar, and age proof as annexures, submit.
Offline Process: Visit the nearest Karmika Seva Kendra (KSK) or a Sanchari Karmika Seva Kendra mobile unit for in-person registration assistance and document submission.
Required Documents: Employment Certificate (Form V(A)/V(B)/V(C)/V(D)) proving 90 days of construction work in the preceding 12 months, Aadhaar Card (self-attested), age proof, Ration Card (non-mandatory).

Issuing Office: Public Health Institute / Private Empanelled Hospital / Citizen Service Centre (enrolment), under Suvarna Arogya Suraksha Trust (State Health Agency)
Department: Karnataka Health & Family Welfare Dept (Suvarna Arogya Suraksha Trust)
Fee: Rs 10 for the standard A4-size enrolment card; Rs 35 for a plastic card; Rs 20 for a reprint
Processing Time: Enrolment/card generation is generally same-day once biometric authentication succeeds
Online Process: Check eligibility and initiate enrolment via pmjay.gov.in ('Am I Eligible') or arogya.karnataka.gov.in; biometric (fingerprint) authentication against the Aadhaar CIDR server is required to complete enrolment, generally done in person at the enrolment point.
Offline Process: Visit the nearest Public Health Institute (PHI), private empanelled hospital, or Citizen Service Centre/B1-K1 centre for one-time biometric enrolment and card issuance on payment of the nominal fee.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, BPL/Priority Household Ration Card or SECC eligibility record (for full 'Eligible Household' benefits), family photograph.

Issuing Office: Insurance company (private/PSU, IRDAI-regulated)
Department: IRDAI (Central regulator; not a government-issued document)
Fee: Varies by insurer/plan (policy premium, not a government fee)
Processing Time: Policy issuance typically 1-7 days after underwriting; e-card usually instant on payment
Online Process: Purchase policy via insurer's website/app or an aggregator; complete KYC and health declaration online; e-health card issued digitally on successful payment.
Offline Process: Purchase through an insurance agent/broker's office; physical/laminated card issued by post after policy issuance.
Required Documents: Policy purchase/proposal form, Aadhaar/PAN for KYC, medical history declaration (and tests for higher sums insured/older applicants).

Issuing Office: Notary Public (appointed under the Notaries Act)
Department: Department of Justice / Karnataka Law Dept
Fee: Notary fee + stamp paper value
Processing Time: Typically same-day at the notary's office
Portal: No official government portal (in-person notarial process)
Online Process: No standard online process for general-purpose affidavits; some limited categories of self-declarations are being moved onto specific department portals.
Offline Process: Visit a Notary Public's office in person (commonly near District/Taluk courts or Sub-Registrar offices in Karnataka) with the drafted affidavit content, valid ID, and appropriate stamp paper for signing and notarisation.
Required Documents: Aadhaar Card, relevant supporting facts/documents being declared, stamp paper of appropriate denomination.
